title: On Lucretia Borgia�s Hair
content:
Borgia, thou once wert almost too august
And high for adoration; now thou �rt dust;
All that remains of thee these plaits unfold,
Calm hair meandering in pellucid gold.
meaning:
Walter Savage Landor's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "On Lucretia Borgia�s Hair"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
